hero.title: Your AI companion,
hero.subtitle: The Great Sage builds Janet — privacy-first, voice-first AI on your devices. No cloud. No subscription.
about.title: About The Great Sage
about.subtitle: Wisdom meets code — we make AI personal and under your control.
about.p1: The Great Sage is the company behind the Janet ecosystem and J.A.N.E.T. Glasses — investor relations, campaigns, and the European glasses vision. We believe everyone deserves an AI companion that respects privacy and runs on hardware they own.
about.p2: Janet is constitutional AI: 20 axioms, teachable runbooks, and cluster-only communication. She grows with you, works offline, and never sends your data to the cloud.
about.roadmap: We're building toward European expansion: Croatia first, then Vienna. EU-made J.A.N.E.T. Glasses, HZZ funding, and a path to GmbH.
companions.title: Janet everywhere
companions.subtitle: From phone to IDE to OS to glasses — one companion, every platform.
companions.calljanetDesc: iPhone & Apple Watch. "Hey Janet!" on the go. Offline, Siri Intents, ready for the App Store.
companions.awakeningDesc: Janet in Cursor IDE. Code with your companion, 100% offline via Continue.dev and janet-seed.
companions.janetosDesc: Full OS for your device. Janet as the interface — Asahi Linux, Apple Silicon, and beyond.
companions.glassesDesc: Live captions and translation. Accessibility-first, offline. Glasses
vision.title: Our vision
vision.p: A world where no one is locked into the cloud — an offline alternative to Siri, Alexa, and Google that you own and control. Janet enhances human connection; she doesn't replace it.
vision.axiomsDef: In our world, an axiom is an immutable rule that governs Janet's behavior — from kindness to privacy to emergency stop.
support.title: Support J.A.N.E.T.
support.subtitle: Help make Janet a reality — the ecosystem, the glasses, and the vision.
support.glassesDesc: Backing the glasses specifically? The Janet Glasses campaign funds live captions and translation on Brilliant Labs Halo — accessibility-first, offline, no subscription.
faq.title: Questions & Answers
faq.subtitle: Common questions about Janet, The Great Sage, and how to get started.
faq.whyJanetA: J.A.N.E.T. stands for Just A Neat Evolving Thinker. It describes what she is: an AI that keeps learning and adapting with you, not a fixed assistant. The name is memorable, works across languages like Japanese, and clearly sets her apart from Siri or Alexa. It's short, easy to say, and feels like a companion rather than a product.
faq.devices: What devices can I use Janet on?
faq.devicesA: Janet runs on your computer (janet-seed), then you connect from your phone (CallJanet for iOS), code editor (Janet Awakening in Cursor), smart home (Home Assistant), or future glasses. There are or will be native apps for iOS, watchOS, macOS, Windows, and Android. The vision includes Meta Quest. Right now you can see what's available at what-can-janet-do.pages.dev. Production-ready deployment is targeted for Q3 2026 if funding is successful.
faq.benefits: What are the benefits of Janet?
faq.benefitsA: Janet is built privacy-first. What that means is that your data stays on your devices and never goes to the cloud. You own your data. Janet works offline so you're not dependent on the internet. She follows a set of 20 rules that define her behavior. She's open source, so people can inspect and improve the code. The glasses add live captions, translation, and memory support, all running locally on your device. And there's no subscription — you pay once for the hardware and can optionally pay for support.
faq.whyCameUp: Why did I come up with Janet?
faq.whyCameUpA: I wanted an alternative to the big tech assistants that rely on the cloud and monetize your data. J.A.N.E.T. — Just A Neat Evolving Thinker — is open, privacy-focused, works offline, and is built with accessibility in mind. The idea is to provide an alternative to Siri, Alexa, and Google with something you can own and trust. It's about giving people real choice instead of closed ecosystems that harvest your data.
faq.howToUseA: You run the core brain (janet-seed) on your computer, then connect from your phone (CallJanet), code editor (Janet Awakening), or smart home. You say "Hey Janet!" to start and talk naturally — she supports both voice and text. When you're finished, say "Thank you, Janet!" or "Good night, Janet." The glasses will show live captions and translation right in your field of view, and all of that stays on your devices — nothing goes to the cloud.
faq.vsOthersA: Janet is meant to replace Siri, Alexa, and Google Assistant. She keeps your data on your devices, not in the cloud. She works offline, follows clear rules, and has no subscription. She's teachable — you can add runbooks and context. The glasses add accessibility features and are designed to be built in Europe in the future. Competitors rely on the cloud, subscriptions, and closed systems. Janet is open source, runs on your hardware, and you keep ownership of everything.
faq.runbooks: What are runbooks and how do they work?
faq.runbooksA: A runbook is like a recipe or checklist for Janet. You write step-by-step instructions instead of code, and she follows them. For example, weather: check internet; if yes, look up the weather and answer in one sentence; if no, say you're offline. Or reminders: ask what and when, save it, then remind the user. Why it matters: no coding, you can change instructions later, you decide what she does in each situation, and she gets better as you add more runbooks. It's teaching her with simple steps instead of programming.
faq.supportMeans: What would support for Janet mean to me?
faq.supportMeansA: Support could mean backing the Janet Glasses campaign on GoFundMe, adopting Janet in your daily life, or contributing to the project. The glasses aim to provide live captions, translation, and memory support — features that matter for accessibility. The campaign target is €5,000, with a deadline of May 1, 2026. Your support would help validate that people want privacy-first, future EU-made AI wearables that don't depend on subscriptions or the cloud.
faq.whyOpenSourceA: Janet is open source, so anyone can inspect, modify, and share the code. That creates trust — no hidden behavior. People can add features, fix bugs, and make sure it's safe. The whole ecosystem, including the core brain (janet-seed), is open. That reduces vendor lock-in and lets the community shape the product instead of a single company.
faq.whyNoSubA: Subscriptions tend to create lock-in and pressure companies to collect more data. Janet uses a one-time purchase model with optional support. The glasses are a single purchase with no monthly fee. That keeps costs predictable and avoids usage caps or paywalls for core features. You buy it once and you own it.
faq.whyPrivacyA: Big tech companies collect and use your data. Janet is built so your data never leaves your devices. There's no cloud storage and no telemetry. We cannot access your conversations. The glasses process your voice locally so it doesn't go anywhere else. Privacy isn't an add-on — it's built into the design from the start, because your words and habits should stay yours.
faq.whyPayA1: You don't have to. If you're tech-savvy, you can run Janet entirely for free — it's open source. Download janet-seed, connect your clients, and you're set. No subscription, no paywall.
faq.whyPayA2: So why pay at all? Two reasons. First: crowdfunding. If you back J.A.N.E.T. Glasses or similar campaigns, you're helping build the ecosystem — hardware, accessibility, EU-made products. Second: service. If you're not into tech, you pay for convenience. J.A.N.E.T. in a box, Janet Server, pre-set glasses — plug-and-play, fully set up. We handle installation and configuration so you get Janet without fiddling with servers or terminals.
faq.whyPayA3: Free if you do it yourself. Pay for support, hardware, or someone else doing the setup for you.
faq.whatGlassesA: Janet Glasses are AI smart glasses that run Janet — live captions, translation, and memory support right in your field of view. Phase 1 is Janet software on Brilliant Labs Halo hardware (JanetXHalo). The long-term goal is J.A.N.E.T. Glasses: our own hardware sourced and built in Europe, same features but Janet-native — offline, no subscription, constitutional AI. They're lightweight, all-day battery, and your voice never leaves your devices.
faq.glassesAccessibility: How do Janet Glasses help with accessibility?
faq.glassesAccessibilityA: Janet Glasses are built for accessibility first. They show live captions so you can read what's being said — for people who are deaf or hard of hearing. They do translation in real time for non-native speakers. And they support memory assist, so you can get gentle reminders and context without sending anything to the cloud. All of that runs locally on your devices. No subscription, no data harvesting.
faq.notDeveloper: I'm not a developer — can I still use Janet?
faq.notDeveloperA: Yes. If you're not into tech, you don't have to run servers yourself. You can back the Janet Glasses campaign and eventually get plug-and-play glasses. Or you can pay for "Janet in a box" or Janet Server — someone sets everything up for you. If you are tech-savvy, you can run it all for free: download janet-seed, connect your phone or editor, and go. Either way, no monthly subscription for core Janet.
faq.sixteenRulesA: Janet follows 20 constitutional axioms — rules that govern how she behaves. They cover things like telling the truth, respecting your privacy, not doing harmful things, and asking you before taking risky actions. One of them is Soul Check: for sensitive actions she'll ask for your confirmation instead of doing them automatically. The full list is on the axioms page. The point is: her behavior is predictable and you're in control.
faq.canDoAnything: Can Janet do anything I ask?
faq.canDoAnythingA: She can do a lot — voice, text, file search, reminders, smart home, coding help. But she won't do things that break her rules. She won't lie, steal your data, or do harmful actions without your explicit confirmation. Soul Check is there so risky or sensitive steps need your okay. You're the one in charge; she's built to support you within clear boundaries.
faq.dataStored: Where is my data stored? Who can see my conversations?
faq.dataStoredA: Your data stays on your devices and in your Janet cluster — the computer and devices you run Janet on. There's no cloud. The team behind Janet can't access your conversations or your voice. The glasses process audio locally. So: you own it, and nobody else can see it unless you choose to share.
faq.cost: How much does it cost to get started?
faq.costA: To get started for free: download janet-seed and connect from your phone or code editor. No subscription. If you want hardware or someone else to set it up, you pay for that — for example backing the Janet Glasses GoFundMe (campaign target €5,000, deadline May 1, 2026) or buying a pre-configured Janet Server. The glasses are one-time purchase, no monthly fee. So: free if you do it yourself; you pay for convenience or to support the ecosystem.
faq.contributeA: Yes. The whole ecosystem is open source (GPL/MIT). The main repo is JANET on GitHub — janet-seed, CallJanet-iOS, Janet Awakening, JanetOS, and more. You can report bugs, suggest features, submit code, or improve docs. There's no vendor lock-in; the community helps shape what Janet becomes.
faq.whyNotChatGPT: Why not just use ChatGPT or Siri?
faq.whyNotChatGPTA: ChatGPT and Siri send your data to the cloud and often require subscriptions or lock you into one company. Janet runs on your hardware, works offline, has no mandatory subscription, and you can teach her with runbooks. Your conversations stay yours. For young adults especially: you get an assistant you actually own, with no hidden data deals or monthly fees.
faq.whoGreatSageA: The Great Sage is the company behind the Janet ecosystem and J.A.N.E.T. Glasses. Janet is the product. The Great Sage is the entity that brings it to market and runs efforts like the GoFundMe for the glasses.
faq.whatsNextA: Short term: production-ready deployment (Q3 2026 if funding works), more apps and platforms, and the Janet Glasses Phase 1 campaign (software on Halo, then a pilot). Next: J.A.N.E.T. Glasses as our own European-sourced hardware — same privacy and no subscription, but built in the EU. The vision is a full ecosystem: OS, iOS, Android, Meta Quest, glasses, and smart home, all offline-first and under your control.
faq.backGlasses: How can I back the glasses?
faq.backGlassesA: Back the Janet Glasses campaign on GoFundMe. The goal is €5,000 by May 1, 2026. Your support funds Halo units for development, JanetXHalo software (live captions and translation), an accessibility pilot, and a landing page for the ecosystem. It's Phase 1 validation: proving that Janet on glasses works before we build our own European hardware. Go to Glasses to support Janet Glasses.
understand.title: I still don't understand
understand.subtitle: We'll explain Janet in a way that fits you — pick your age and how you like to learn.
understand.pageTitle: I still don't understand · The Great Sage
understand.metaDesc: We explain Janet in a way that fits you. Pick your age and how you like to learn — simple or technical.
understand.subhead: We'll explain Janet in a way that fits you. Choose your age and how you like to learn.
understand.agePrompt: Please select your age group so we can explain it better to you:
understand.tabsLabel: How would you like it explained?
understand.tabNoidea: I have no IDEA ABOUT TECHNOLOGY
understand.placeholder: Select your age and how you'd like the explanation above, then we'll show you a version that fits you.
understand.child.geek.title: Janet for kids (technical)
understand.child.geek.body: Janet is an AI that runs on your family's own computer or phone instead of in the cloud. She listens when you say "Hey Janet!" and answers using rules (axioms) so she stays safe and predictable. No app store skills, no exposed servers — just your device. Parents can teach her with runbooks (step-by-step instructions) so she does exactly what you allow.
understand.child.noidea.title: Janet for kids (simple)
understand.child.noidea.body: Janet is like a friendly robot that lives on your tablet or your family's computer. When you say "Hey Janet!" she talks back — and everything stays at your house. Nothing goes to the internet. Your parents can teach her what she's allowed to do. She follows 20 rules so she's safe and nice.
understand.teen.geek.title: Janet for teens (technical)
understand.teen.geek.body: Janet is a constitutional AI companion: 20 axioms, local inference (Neural Engine on device or janet-seed on your machine), and cluster-only state. No plugin marketplace, no gateway — so no OpenClaw-style attack surface. You get voice/text, runbooks, and optional clients (CallJanet, Awakening, glasses) without cloud or subscription.
understand.teen.noidea.title: Janet for teens (simple)
understand.teen.noidea.body: Janet is an AI you actually own. She runs on your phone or computer, not in some company's cloud. Say "Hey Janet!" to talk; she follows clear rules and doesn't send your stuff anywhere. No monthly fee, no sketchy apps — just you and your device. You can even teach her new habits with simple instructions.
understand.youngAdult.geek.title: Janet for young adults (technical)
understand.youngAdult.geek.body: Janet is an offline-first, constitutional AI stack: janet-seed as the brain, WebSocket clients (CallJanet, Janet Awakening, JanetOS), 20 axioms, runbook-driven behavior. No cloud dependency, no untrusted plugin ecosystem, no exposed gateway — your data stays in your cluster. LLM can be local (Ollama, Neural Engine) or your chosen provider; orchestration is on your hardware.
understand.youngAdult.noidea.title: Janet for young adults (simple)
understand.youngAdult.noidea.body: Janet is your AI companion that runs on your devices — phone, laptop, even glasses. She works offline, doesn't sell your data, and has no subscription. You say "Hey Janet!" to start; she helps with reminders, answers, and more, all while following 20 rules so she's predictable and safe. You own it; no big tech in the middle.
understand.adult.geek.title: Janet for adults (technical)
understand.adult.geek.body: Janet is a privacy-first AI runtime: constitutional (20 axioms), cluster-scoped state, local or self-hosted LLM, runbooks for behavior. Clients include CallJanet (iOS/watchOS), Janet Awakening (Cursor), JanetOS, and J.A.N.E.T. Glasses. No plugin marketplace, no cloud gateway — reduces supply-chain and exposure risk compared to agent platforms that rely on third-party skills and exposed services.
understand.adult.noidea.title: Janet for adults (simple)
understand.adult.noidea.body: Janet is an AI assistant that stays on your devices. No cloud, no subscription, no data harvesting. You can use her on your phone, computer, or (coming) glasses. She follows 20 clear rules and you can teach her with simple step-by-step instructions. Your conversations and data never leave your control — built for people who want an alternative to Siri, Alexa, or Google.
understand.senior.geek.title: Janet for seniors (technical)
understand.senior.geek.body: Janet is a constitutional, offline-first AI companion with cluster-only state and runbook-driven behavior. No plugin ecosystem or exposed gateway — so no dependency on third-party skills or open ports. Clients (CallJanet, Awakening, glasses) connect to janet-seed; inference can be fully local (Ollama, Neural Engine). Suited for users who want control and auditability without cloud or subscription.
understand.senior.noidea.title: Janet for seniors (simple)
understand.senior.noidea.body: Janet is a friendly AI that lives on your phone or computer. You say "Hey Janet!" to talk to her. She doesn't use the internet to work (after setup), so your words stay private. There's no monthly fee and no confusing apps from the store — just one companion that follows clear rules. Great for reminders, questions, and help without giving your data to big companies.

janet.title: Ask Janet
janet.jackCta: If you wish; we can use your web-browser and your device to demonstrate the power of JANET
janet.welcome: Hi! I'm Janet. Ask me about The Great Sage — our mission, privacy-first AI, or J.A.N.E.T. Glasses. Everything runs right here in your browser.
janet.fallbackBody: The Great Sage builds Janet — privacy-first, voice-first AI on your devices. No cloud. No subscription. Constitutional AI, open source. J.A.N.E.T. Glasses. Your data stays yours.
janet.fallbackHint: Try Chrome, Edge, or Safari for the full in-browser AI experience.
janet.fallbackHintSafari: Safari has limited WebGPU support (iOS 18.2+, macOS Safari 18.2+). Use Chrome or Edge for the full in-browser AI. Or try the CallJanet app on iOS.
janet.whoMadeYou: The Great Sage builds Janet — the company behind this ecosystem and J.A.N.E.T. Glasses. Janet is the product; The Great Sage is the entity that brings it to market.
janet.whatCanYouDo: I can do a lot — voice, text, file search, reminders, smart home, coding help. I follow 20 constitutional axioms, so I won't lie, steal your data, or do harmful actions without your explicit confirmation. You're in charge; I'm built to support you within clear boundaries.
janet.myName: I'm Janet. J.A.N.E.T. stands for Just A Neat Evolving Thinker — an AI that keeps learning and adapting with you, not a fixed assistant.
